LAW AND MOTION 302, NO APPEARANCE ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: CROSS DEFENDANT CHICAGO TITLE COMPANY'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT IS CONTINUED ON THE COURT'S OWN MOTION TO OCTOBER 19, 2012 TO GIVE THE PARTIES AN OPPORTUNITY TO TRY TO RESOLVE THEIR DISPUTES BEFORE EXPENDING ANY FURTHER RESOURCES ON THIS CASE. PANG LY, AN ATTORNEY EMPLOYED BY THE COURT WHO HAS ASSISTED MANY PARTIES IN SETTLING THEIR CASES, WILL BE CONTACTING THE PARTIES TO ARRANGE FOR A COURT-SPONSORED SETTLEMENT CONFERENCE. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; COURT REPORTER: LAVENA WARD, CSR # 7077 =(302/HEK)

JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, NOT REPORTED.

LAW AND MOTION 302, CROSS-DEFENDANT CHICAGO TITLE COMPANY'S MOTION TO STRIKE PORTIONS OF THE CROSS-COMPLAINT - A R G U E D; THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. MOTION TO STRIKE IS DENIED. CROSS-COMPLAINANTS HAVE STATED SUFFICIENT FACTS TO SUPPORT A CLAIM FOR PUNITIVE DAMAGES. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; COURT REPORTER: SUSAN CROZIER, CSR # 4761

LAW AND MOTION 302, THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANTS NEIL EISENBERG AND SUSAN EISENBERG'S MOTION TO DISMISS AND TO STRIKE COMPLAINT IS DENIED. A MOTION TO STRIKE IS NOT THE PROPER VEHICLE TO DISQUALIFY WELLS FARGO'S COUNSEL. FURTHER, AS A CALIFORNIA LICENSED ATTORNEY, MR. LEE IS BOUND BY THE FIDUCIARY AND ETHICAL DUTIES TO HIS CLIENT REGARDLESS OF HIS ASSOCIATION WITH FIDELITY NATIONAL LAW GROUP. THE VERIFICATION SUBSTANTIALLY COMPLIES WITH SECTION 446(A). ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD E. KAHN; COURT REPORTER: LAVENA WARD CSR #7077
SUBSTITUTION OF ATTORNEY: LASTOWSKI, JULIAN THOMAS SUBSTITUTED FOR LEE, WILLIAM AS ATTORNEY FOR WELLS FARGO BANK, N.A.

LAW AND MOTION 302, THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. CROSS DEFENDANT CHICAGO TITLE COMPANY'S MOTION TO STRIKE CROSS COMPLAINT IS GRANTED, WITH 10 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND. CROSS-COMPLAINANTS ARE GRANTED LEAVE TO AMEND, IF THEY CAN DO SO IN GOOD FAITH, TO PLEAD SPECIFIC FACTS TO SUPPORT THEIR CLAIMS OF FRAUD, OPPRESSION OR MALICE. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE CURTIS KARNOW; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; NOT REPORTED.

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Aug-09-2012 Continued To Oct-24-12 At 02:00 P.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Aug-01-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. DAVID M. GONDEN OF TROMBADORE & GONDEN LAW GROUP, TEL. NO. 439-8372 APPEARING FOR THE CROSS-DEFENDANT;
NO OTHER APPEARANCES ON THE MATTER.

RULING - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. CROSS-DEFENDANT CHICAGO TITLE COMPANY'S DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT - As to the 1st, 2nd and 4th causes of action: statute of limitations: demurrers sustained with leave to amend. To the extent cross complainants are relying on the delayed discovery rule, they must plead with specificity the time and manner of discovery, and why they did not make the discovery earlier.
1st cause of action: Otherwise overruled, as the confidential relationship is sufficiently alleged ("escrow holder").
2nd cause of action: otherwise sustained with leave to plead each element of the claim with specificity (the 'who, what, where, and when'). In this context, the court is interested solely in allegations against cross defendant, not other entities. Among other things, consequent damages must be specifically alleged, which may pose issues given the offer of Fidelity National to compensate (cross complaint 16 e).
3rd cause of action: Otherwise overruled. See discussion under first cause of action.
4th cause of action: Otherwise sustained with leave to plead each element including damages. See comments above re: damages.
6th cause of action: sustained with leave to plead appropriate facts in support of the claim.
7th cause of action: sustained with leave to plead a restitution claim, specifying among other things the nature of the restitution sought.
Demurrers otherwise overruled.
Cross complainants have 10 days leave to amend if they can do so in good faith. Cross-Defendant's request for judicial notice is unopposed and is accordingly granted. Cross-Complainants' request for judicial notice is denied. Copies of a web-site are not judicially noticeable, at least not for the truth of their content. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge Curtis Karnow; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Not reported.
=(302/CK)

MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Mar-14-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. WILLIAM LEE, TEL. NO. (925) 930-9550 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
NO OTHER APPEARANCES ON THE MATTER.

RULING - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTS NEIL EISENBERG AND SUSAN EISENBERG'S MOTION TO DISMISS AND TO STRIKE COMPLAINT IS Denied. A motion to strike is not the proper vehicle to disqualify Wells Fargo's counsel. Further, as a California licensed attorney, Mr. Lee is bound by the fiduciary and ethical duties to his client regardless of his association with Fidelity National Law Group. The verification substantially complies with section 446(a). ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Harold E. Kahn; Court Reporter: LaVena Ward CSR #7077 = (302/HEK)
